nodes.x: PRAISE ye the LORD. Praise ye the name of the LORD; praise [him], O ye servants of the LORD.
of RAISE ye the Lord.’ Praise ye the name of the Lord ; praise him, Oye servants of the-Lord.
Hallelujah, meaning praise ye the Lord: that is, praise Jehovah - there are 4 Alleluias in this section.
Praise ye the Lord: Praise him who all things did create; Praise him who all things did redeem; Exalt his name and seek his faceO praise ye the Lord.
Ye that stand in the house of the LORD, in the courts of the house of our God,
Praise the LORD; for the LORD [is] good: sing praises unto his name; for [it is] pleasant.
For the LORD hath chosen Jacob unto himself, [and] Israel for his peculiar treasure.

Thus all that Jacob did contained a deep symbolic purpose. Further it is written: AND JACOB TOOK RODS OF FRESH POPLAR, ETC. R. Eleazar discoursed here on the verse: For the Lord hath chosen Jacob unto himself and Israelfor his own treasure (Ps. 135, 4). ‘From the actual words of the original’, he said, ‘we could not tell whether it was the Lord who chose Jacob or vice-versa. That the former is…
For I know that the LORD [is] great, and [that] our Lord [is] above all gods.
{for we are about to destroy this place,} for the cry of it before the Lord is great, and the Lord hath sent us to destroy it.
Whatsoever the LORD pleased, [that] did he in heaven, and in earth, in the seas, and all deep places.
He causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth; he maketh lightnings for the rain; he bringeth the wind out of his treasuries.
Who smote the firstborn of Egypt, both of man and beast.

And it came to pass in the middle of the night, that the Lord smote all the firstborn in the land of Egypt (Exod. 12:29). The Creator of the night divided the night precisely. The Lord smote all the first born. It was the Lord Himself who smote the firstborn and not His emissary (Moses). In fact, even though a man was elsewhere, and his firstborn was in Egypt, he died. THE TIMES.

“Because all the firstborn-are mine; for on the day that I smote air the first- born in the land of Egypt, I hallowed unto me all the firstborn in Israel, both man and beast; mine shall they be: lam the Lord.” Here, then, we perceive that when the Lord smote the firstborn of Egypt from the child of Pharaoh on the throne to the child of the captive in the dungeon, the Lord saved…
[Who] sent tokens and wonders into the midst of thee, O Egypt, upon Pharaoh, and upon all his servants.
Who smote great nations, and slew mighty kings;
LXIII. A last despairing appeal by the kings and mighty. They justify God’s judgement. 1, the mighty and the kings a, ‘kings’ aehknvw, ‘ mighty kings’ dcdfilopxry,ayb.
Sihon king of the Amorites, and Og king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan:

AFTER HE HAD SMITTEN SIHON, THE KING OF THE AMORITES, WHO ‘DWELT’ IN HESHBON — for Heshbon was not his, but he conquered it from the king of Moab and he built it to be his royal capital. Similarly, and Og the king of Bashan, who ‘dwelt’ in Ashtaroth, at Edrei39In Verse 4 before us. means that it was previously the land of Rephaim40Further, 3:13. [and then it was occupied by the king of the…
And gave their land [for] an heritage, an heritage unto Israel his people.
Thy name, O LORD, [endureth] for ever; [and] thy memorial, O LORD, throughout all generations.
For the LORD will judge his people, and he will repent himself concerning his servants.
The idols of the heathen [are] silver and gold, the work of men's hands.
Silver and gold. Hezekiah had to give up wealth by his foolish display of it
They have mouths, but they speak not; eyes have they, but they see not;
They have ears, but they hear not; neither is there [any] breath in their mouths.
They that make them are like unto them: [so is] every one that trusteth in them.
7 Or, if there be any among you that shall make one like unto it, then ye are justified in saying that ye do not know that they are true;
Bless the LORD, O house of Israel: bless the LORD, O house of Aaron:

“O bless us, Lord! O bless us, Lord!
    O come in all yer might;
    Unless yer’ll come an’ bless us, Lord,
    We’ll wrestle hyear all night.
And now see what he hath wrought with you, And give him thanks with your whole mouth, And bless the Lord of righteousness,
Bless the LORD, O house of Levi: ye that fear the LORD, bless the LORD.
Blessed be the LORD out of Zion, which dwelleth at Jerusalem. Praise ye the LORD.
JST Blessed be the Lord out of Zion; Blessed be the Lord out of Jerusalem. Praise ye the Lord.

He further discoursed on the verse: “Blessed be the Lord out of Zion, who dwelleth at Jerusalem, hallelukah” (Ps. 135, 21). He said: ‘Is the Lord blessed from Zion? Is He not blessed from the recondite supernal stream? What it means, however, is that the Lord is blessed when the Moon is illumined by the Sun and they draw near one another.

Now, in the command “On this wise (KoH) shall ye bless”, it is signified that for the sake of Israel the very KoH is to be blessed by the priest, so that tsrael will be blessed below, and thus blessing will be diffused through the universe. As for the time to come, it is written, “The Lord bless thee out of Zion…” (Ps. 134, 3); as well as, “Blessed be the Lord out of Zion, who dwelleth at…
